what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG outline icon showing a video window: a rectangular screen with a play triangle and a camera projection shape on the right.
observations: Uses an inline SVG with viewBox 0 0 24 24.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round caps and joins.
Contains one rounded rectangle and two path elements.
The play triangle is drawn as a stroked closed path.
The right-side video camera shape is drawn as a stroked path connected to the window edge.
standouts: Combines playback and video-camera cues in a single icon.
Uses currentColor so the icon inherits text or parent color.
conventions: Follows scalable outline icon conventions with viewBox, currentColor stroke, no fill, and rounded stroke styling.
Keeps geometry in simple SVG primitives without embedded styles or scripts.
Defines the asset as a compact single-line SVG.
concerns: No title or desc element is present, so accessible naming would need to come from the embedding context.
notes: A reusable outline SVG icon for a video window or video playback interface.
questions: Should the play triangle remain stroke-only, or was a filled play mark intended?
Is accessible labeling expected to be supplied by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the filename: the file defines a video-window style SVG icon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a small themeable interface icon representing video content, playback, or a video window.
novelty: Combines a window frame, play symbol, and camera projection into one compact 24px icon.
contribution: Contributes a compact currentColor outline media icon that a fused system would keep for video-window, playback, or video interface affordances.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG outline icon depicting a video window with a play triangle and side camera shape.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing video playback or a video window in a reusable design-system icon asset.
